NKJV.1: We then who are strong ought to bear with the scruples of the weak, and not to please ourselves.
NKJV.2: Let each of us please his neighbor for his good, leading to edification.
NKJV.3: For even Christ did not please Himself; but as it is written, “The reproaches of those who reproached You fell on Me.”
NKJV.4: For whatever things were written before were written for our learning, that we through the patience and comfort of the Scriptures might have hope.
NKJV.5: Now may the God of patience and comfort grant you to be like-minded toward one another, according to Christ Jesus,
NKJV.6: that you may with one mind and one mouth glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NKJV.7: Therefore receive one another, just as Christ also received us, to the glory of God.
NKJV.8: Now I say that Jesus Christ has become a servant to the circumcision for the truth of God, to confirm the promises made to the fathers,
NKJV.9: and that the Gentiles might glorify God for His mercy, as it is written: “For this reason I will confess to You among the Gentiles, And sing to Your name.”
NKJV.10: And again he says:  “Rejoice, O Gentiles, with His people!”
NKJV.11: And again: “Praise the LORD, all you Gentiles! Laud Him, all you peoples!”
NKJV.12: And again, Isaiah says:  “There shall be a root of Jesse; And He who shall rise to reign over the Gentiles, In Him the Gentiles shall hope.”
NKJV.13: Now may the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that you may abound in hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
NKJV.14: Now I myself am confident concerning you, my brethren, that you also are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, able also to admonish one another.
NKJV.15: Nevertheless, brethren, I have written more boldly to you on some points, as reminding you, because of the grace given to me by God,
NKJV.16: that I might be a minister of Jesus Christ to the Gentiles, ministering the gospel of God, that the offering of the Gentiles might be acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.
NKJV.17: Therefore I have reason to glory in Christ Jesus in the things which pertain to God.
NKJV.18: For I will not dare to speak of any of those things which Christ has not accomplished through me, in word and deed, to make the Gentiles obedient—
NKJV.19: in mighty signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit of God, so that from Jerusalem and round about to Illyricum I have fully preached the gospel of Christ.
NKJV.20: And so I have made it my aim to preach the gospel, not where Christ was named, lest I should build on another man’s foundation,
NKJV.21: but as it is written:  “To whom He was not announced, they shall see; And those who have not heard shall understand.”
NKJV.22: For this reason I also have been much hindered from coming to you.
NKJV.23: But now no longer having a place in these parts, and having a great desire these many years to come to you,
NKJV.24: whenever I journey to Spain, I shall come to you. For I hope to see you on my journey, and to be helped on my way there by you, if first I may enjoy your company for a while.
NKJV.25: But now I am going to Jerusalem to minister to the saints.
NKJV.26: For it pleased those from Macedonia and Achaia to make a certain contribution for the poor among the saints who are in Jerusalem.
NKJV.27: It pleased them indeed, and they are their debtors. For if the Gentiles have been partakers of their spiritual things, their duty is also to minister to them in material things.
NKJV.28: Therefore, when I have performed this and have sealed to them this fruit, I shall go by way of you to Spain.
NKJV.29: But I know that when I come to you, I shall come in the fullness of the blessing of the gospel of Christ.
NKJV.30: Now I beg you, brethren, through the Lord Jesus Christ, and through the love of the Spirit, that you strive together with me in prayers to God for me,
NKJV.31: that I may be delivered from those in Judea who do not believe, and that my service for Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints,
NKJV.32: that I may come to you with joy by the will of God, and may be refreshed together with you.
NKJV.33: Now the God of peace be with you all. Amen.

ESV.1: We who are strong have an obligation to bear with the failings of the weak, and not to please ourselves.
ESV.2: Let each of us please his neighbor for his good, to build him up.
ESV.3: For Christ did not please himself, but as it is written, “The reproaches of those who reproached you fell on me.”
ESV.4: For whatever was written in former days was written for our instruction, that through endurance and through the encouragement of the Scriptures we might have hope.
ESV.5: May the God of endurance and encouragement grant you to live in such harmony with one another, in accord with Christ Jesus,
ESV.6: that together you may with one voice glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
ESV.7: Therefore welcome one another as Christ has welcomed you, for the glory of God.
ESV.8: For I tell you that Christ became a servant to the circumcised to show God's truthfulness, in order to confirm the promises given to the patriarchs,
ESV.9: and in order that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, “Therefore I will praise you among the Gentiles, and sing to your name.”
ESV.10: And again it is said, “Rejoice, O Gentiles, with his people.”
ESV.11: And again, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and let all the peoples extol him.”
ESV.12: And again Isaiah says,  “The root of Jesse will come, even he who arises to rule the Gentiles; in him will the Gentiles hope.”
ESV.13: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that by the power of the Holy Spirit you may abound in hope.
ESV.14: I myself am satisfied about you, my brothers,  that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge and able to instruct one another.
ESV.15: But on some points I have written to you very boldly by way of reminder, because of the grace given me by God
ESV.16: to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles in the priestly service of the gospel of God, so that the offering of the Gentiles may be acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.
ESV.17: In Christ Jesus, then, I have reason to be proud of my work for God.
ESV.18: For I will not venture to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me to bring the Gentiles to obedience — by word and deed,
ESV.19: by the power of signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit of God — so that from Jerusalem and all the way around to Illyricum I have fulfilled the ministry of the gospel of Christ;
ESV.20: and thus I make it my ambition to preach the gospel, not where Christ has already been named, lest I build on someone else's foundation,
ESV.21: but as it is written, “Those who have never been told of him will see, and those who have never heard will understand.”
ESV.22: This is the reason why I have so often been hindered from coming to you.
ESV.23: But now, since I no longer have any room for work in these regions, and since I have longed for many years to come to you,
ESV.24: I hope to see you in passing as I go to Spain, and to be helped on my journey there by you, once I have enjoyed your company for a while.
ESV.25: At present, however, I am going to Jerusalem bringing aid to the saints.
ESV.26: For Macedonia and Achaia have been pleased to make some contribution for the poor among the saints at Jerusalem.
ESV.27: For they were pleased to do it, and indeed they owe it to them. For if the Gentiles have come to share in their spiritual blessings, they ought also to be of service to them in material blessings.
ESV.28: When therefore I have completed this and have delivered to them what has been collected,  I will leave for Spain by way of you.
ESV.29: I know that when I come to you I will come in the fullness of the blessing  of Christ.
ESV.30: I appeal to you, brothers, by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the Spirit, to strive together with me in your prayers to God on my behalf,
ESV.31: that I may be delivered from the unbelievers in Judea, and that my service for Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints,
ESV.32: so that by God's will I may come to you with joy and be refreshed in your company.
ESV.33: May the God of peace be with you all. Amen.

NASB.1: Now we who are strong ought to bear the weaknesses of those without strength and not [just] please ourselves.
NASB.2: Each of us is to please his neighbor for his good, to his edification.
NASB.3: For even Christ did not please Himself; but as it is written, 'THE REPROACHES OF THOSE WHO REPROACHED YOU FELL ON ME.'
NASB.4: For whatever was written in earlier times was written for our instruction, so that through perseverance and the encouragement of the Scriptures we might have hope.
NASB.5: Now may the God who gives perseverance and encouragement grant you to be of the same mind with one another according to Christ Jesus,
NASB.6: so that with one accord you may with one voice glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NASB.7: Therefore, accept one another, just as Christ also accepted us to the glory of God.
NASB.8: For I say that Christ has become a servant to the circumcision on behalf of the truth of God to confirm the promises [given] to the fathers,
NASB.9: and for the Gentiles to glorify God for His mercy; as it is written, 'THEREFORE I WILL GIVE PRAISE TO YOU AMONG THE GENTILES, AND I WILL SING TO YOUR NAME.'
NASB.10: Again he says, 'REJOICE, O GENTILES, WITH HIS PEOPLE.'
NASB.11: And again, 'PRAISE THE LORD ALL YOU GENTILES, AND LET ALL THE PEOPLES PRAISE HIM.'
NASB.12: Again Isaiah says, 'THERE SHALL COME THE ROOT OF JESSE, AND HE WHO ARISES TO RULE OVER THE GENTILES, IN HIM SHALL THE GENTILES HOPE.'
NASB.13: Now may the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that you will abound in hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
NASB.14: And concerning you, my brethren, I myself also am convinced that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge and able also to admonish one another.
NASB.15: But I have written very boldly to you on some points so as to remind you again, because of the grace that was given me from God,
NASB.16: to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles, ministering as a priest the gospel of God, so that [my] offering of the Gentiles may become acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.
NASB.17: Therefore in Christ Jesus I have found reason for boasting in things pertaining to God.
NASB.18: For I will not presume to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me, resulting in the obedience of the Gentiles by word and deed,
NASB.19: in the power of signs and wonders, in the power of the Spirit; so that from Jerusalem and round about as far as Illyricum I have fully preached the gospel of Christ.
NASB.20: And thus I aspired to preach the gospel, not where Christ was [already] named, so that I would not build on another man's foundation;
NASB.21: but as it is written, 'THEY WHO HAD NO NEWS OF HIM SHALL SEE, AND THEY WHO HAVE NOT HEARD SHALL UNDERSTAND.'
NASB.22: For this reason I have often been prevented from coming to you;
NASB.23: but now, with no further place for me in these regions, and since I have had for many years a longing to come to you
NASB.24: whenever I go to Spain-- for I hope to see you in passing, and to be helped on my way there by you, when I have first enjoyed your company for a while--
NASB.25: but now, I am going to Jerusalem serving the saints.
NASB.26: For Macedonia and Achaia have been pleased to make a contribution for the poor among the saints in Jerusalem.
NASB.27: Yes, they were pleased [to do so], and they are indebted to them. For if the Gentiles have shared in their spiritual things, they are indebted to minister to them also in material things.
NASB.28: Therefore, when I have finished this, and have put my seal on this fruit of theirs, I will go on by way of you to Spain.
NASB.29: I know that when I come to you, I will come in the fullness of the blessing of Christ.
NASB.30: Now I urge you, brethren, by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the Spirit, to strive together with me in your prayers to God for me,
NASB.31: that I may be rescued from those who are disobedient in Judea, and [that] my service for Jerusalem may prove acceptable to the saints;
NASB.32: so that I may come to you in joy by the will of God and find [refreshing] rest in your company.
NASB.33: Now the God of peace be with you all. Amen.

NLT.1: We who are strong must be considerate of those who are sensitive about things like this. We must not just please ourselves.
NLT.2: We should help others do what is right and build them up in the Lord.
NLT.3: For even Christ didn’t live to please himself. As the Scriptures say, “The insults of those who insult you, O God, have fallen on me.”
NLT.4: Such things were written in the Scriptures long ago to teach us. And the Scriptures give us hope and encouragement as we wait patiently for God’s promises to be fulfilled.
NLT.5: May God, who gives this patience and encouragement, help you live in complete harmony with each other, as is fitting for followers of Christ Jesus.
NLT.6: Then all of you can join together with one voice, giving praise and glory to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NLT.7: Therefore, accept each other just as Christ has accepted you so that God will be given glory.
NLT.8: Remember that Christ came as a servant to the Jews to show that God is true to the promises he made to their ancestors.
NLT.9:
He also came so that the Gentiles might give glory to God for his mercies to them. That is what the psalmist meant when he wrote: “For this, I will praise you among the Gentiles;
I will sing praises to your name.”
NLT.10:
And in another place it is written, “Rejoice with his people,
you Gentiles.”
NLT.11:
And yet again, “Praise the LORD, all you Gentiles.
Praise him, all you people of the earth.”
NLT.12:
And in another place Isaiah said, “The heir to David’s throne will come,
and he will rule over the Gentiles.
They will place their hope on him.”
NLT.13: I pray that God, the source of hope, will fill you completely with joy and peace because you trust in him. Then you will overflow with confident hope through the power of the Holy Spirit.
NLT.14: I am fully convinced, my dear brothers and sisters, that you are full of goodness. You know these things so well you can teach each other all about them.
NLT.15: Even so, I have been bold enough to write about some of these points, knowing that all you need is this reminder. For by God’s grace,
NLT.16: I am a special messenger from Christ Jesus to you Gentiles. I bring you the Good News so that I might present you as an acceptable offering to God, made holy by the Holy Spirit.
NLT.17: So I have reason to be enthusiastic about all Christ Jesus has done through me in my service to God.
NLT.18: Yet I dare not boast about anything except what Christ has done through me, bringing the Gentiles to God by my message and by the way I worked among them.
NLT.19: They were convinced by the power of miraculous signs and wonders and by the power of God’s Spirit. In this way, I have fully presented the Good News of Christ from Jerusalem all the way to Illyricum.
NLT.20: My ambition has always been to preach the Good News where the name of Christ has never been heard, rather than where a church has already been started by someone else.
NLT.21:
I have been following the plan spoken of in the Scriptures, where it says, “Those who have never been told about him will see,
and those who have never heard of him will understand.”
NLT.22: In fact, my visit to you has been delayed so long because I have been preaching in these places.
NLT.23: But now I have finished my work in these regions, and after all these long years of waiting, I am eager to visit you.
NLT.24: I am planning to go to Spain, and when I do, I will stop off in Rome. And after I have enjoyed your fellowship for a little while, you can provide for my journey.
NLT.25: But before I come, I must go to Jerusalem to take a gift to the believers there.
NLT.26: For you see, the believers in Macedonia and Achaia have eagerly taken up an offering for the poor among the believers in Jerusalem.
NLT.27: They were glad to do this because they feel they owe a real debt to them. Since the Gentiles received the spiritual blessings of the Good News from the believers in Jerusalem, they feel the least they can do in return is to help them financially.
NLT.28: As soon as I have delivered this money and completed this good deed of theirs, I will come to see you on my way to Spain.
NLT.29: And I am sure that when I come, Christ will richly bless our time together.
NLT.30: Dear brothers and sisters, I urge you in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to join in my struggle by praying to God for me. Do this because of your love for me, given to you by the Holy Spirit.
NLT.31: Pray that I will be rescued from those in Judea who refuse to obey God. Pray also that the believers there will be willing to accept the donation I am taking to Jerusalem.
NLT.32: Then, by the will of God, I will be able to come to you with a joyful heart, and we will be an encouragement to each other.
NLT.33: And now may God, who gives us his peace, be with you all. Amen.

WEB.1: Now we who are strong ought to bear the weaknesses of the weak, and not to please ourselves.
WEB.2: Let each one of us please his neighbor for that which is good, to be building him up.
WEB.3: For even Christ didn’t please himself. But, as it is written, “The reproaches of those who reproached you fell on me.”
WEB.4: For whatever things were written before were written for our learning, that through perseverance and through encouragement of the Scriptures we might have hope.
WEB.5: Now the God of perseverance and of encouragement grant you to be of the same mind one with another according to Christ Jesus,
WEB.6: that with one accord you may with one mouth glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
WEB.7: Therefore accept one another, even as Christ also accepted you,  to the glory of God.
WEB.8: Now I say that Christ has been made a servant of the circumcision for the truth of God, that he might confirm the promises given to the fathers,
WEB.9: and that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, “Therefore will I give praise to you among the Gentiles, and sing to your name.”
WEB.10: Again he says, “Rejoice, you Gentiles, with his people.”
WEB.11: Again, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles! Let all the peoples praise him.”
WEB.12: Again, Isaiah says, “There will be the root of Jesse, he who arises to rule over the Gentiles; in him the Gentiles will hope.”
WEB.13: Now may the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that you may abound in hope, in the power of the Holy Spirit.
WEB.14: I myself am also persuaded about you, my brothers,  that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, able also to admonish others.
WEB.15: But I write the more boldly to you in part, as reminding you, because of the grace that was given to me by God,
WEB.16: that I should be a servant of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles, serving as a priest of the Good News of God, that the offering up of the Gentiles might be made acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.
WEB.17: I have therefore my boasting in Christ Jesus in things pertaining to God.
WEB.18: For I will not dare to speak of any things except those which Christ worked through me, for the obedience of the Gentiles, by word and deed,
WEB.19: in the power of signs and wonders, in the power of God’s Spirit; so that from Jerusalem, and around as far as to Illyricum, I have fully preached the Good News of Christ;
WEB.20: yes, making it my aim to preach the Good News, not where Christ was already named, that I might not build on another’s foundation.
WEB.21: But, as it is written, “They will see, to whom no news of him came. They who haven’t heard will understand.”
WEB.22: Therefore also I was hindered these many times from coming to you,
WEB.23: but now, no longer having any place in these regions, and having these many years a longing to come to you,
WEB.24: whenever I travel to Spain, I will come to you. For I hope to see you on my journey, and to be helped on my way there by you, if first I may enjoy your company for a while.
WEB.25: But now, I say, I am going to Jerusalem, serving the saints.
WEB.26: For it has been the good pleasure of Macedonia and Achaia to make a certain contribution for the poor among the saints who are at Jerusalem.
WEB.27: Yes, it has been their good pleasure, and they are their debtors. For if the Gentiles have been made partakers of their spiritual things, they owe it to them also to serve them in fleshly things.
WEB.28: When therefore I have accomplished this, and have sealed to them this fruit, I will go on by way of you to Spain.
WEB.29: I know that, when I come to you, I will come in the fullness of the blessing of the Good News of Christ.
WEB.30: Now I beg you, brothers, by our Lord Jesus Christ, and by the love of the Spirit, that you strive together with me in your prayers to God for me,
WEB.31: that I may be delivered from those who are disobedient in Judea, and that my service which I have for Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints;
WEB.32: that I may come to you in joy through the will of God, and together with you, find rest.
WEB.33: Now the God of peace be with you all. Amen.

CSB17.1: Now we who are strong have an obligation to bear the weaknesses of those without strength, <sup>ⓐ</sup> and not to please ourselves.
CSB17.2: Each one of us is to please his neighbor for his good, to build him up. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.3: For even Christ did not please himself. ⓒ On the contrary, as it is written, The insults of those who insult you have fallen on me. [1] ⓓ
CSB17.4: For whatever was written in the past was written for our instruction, <sup>ⓔ</sup> so that we may have hope through endurance and through the encouragement from the Scriptures.
CSB17.5: Now may the God who gives <sup>[2]</sup> endurance and encouragement <sup>ⓕ</sup> grant you to live in harmony with one another, <sup>ⓖ</sup> according to Christ Jesus,
CSB17.6: so that you may glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ <sup>ⓗ</sup> with one mind and one voice.
CSB17.7: Therefore accept one another, <sup>ⓘ</sup> just as Christ also accepted you, to the glory of God.
CSB17.8: For I say that Christ became a servant of the circumcised <sup>[3]</sup> on behalf of God’s truth, <sup>ⓙ</sup> to confirm the promises to the fathers, <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.9:
and so that Gentiles ⓛ may glorify God for his mercy. ⓜ As it is written, Therefore I will praise you among the Gentiles,
and I will sing praise to your name. [4] ⓝ
CSB17.10: Again it says, Rejoice, you Gentiles, with his people! [5] ⓐ
CSB17.11:
And again, Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles;
let all the peoples praise him! [6] ⓑ
CSB17.12:
And again, Isaiah says, The root of Jesse ⓒ will appear,
the one who rises to rule the Gentiles;
the Gentiles will hope in him. [7] ⓓ
CSB17.13: Now may the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace as you believe <sup>ⓔ</sup> so that you may overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.14: My brothers and sisters, I myself am convinced about you that you also are full of goodness, <sup>ⓖ</sup> filled with all knowledge, <sup>ⓗ</sup> and able to instruct one another.
CSB17.15: Nevertheless, I have written to remind you more boldly on some points <sup>[8]</sup> because of the grace given me by God <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.16: to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles, <sup>ⓙ</sup> serving as a priest of the gospel of God. <sup>ⓚ</sup> My purpose is that the Gentiles may be an acceptable offering, <sup>ⓛ</sup> sanctified by the Holy Spirit.
CSB17.17: Therefore I have reason to boast in Christ Jesus <sup>ⓜ</sup> regarding what pertains to God. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.18: For I would not dare say anything except what Christ has accomplished through me <sup>ⓐ</sup> by word and deed for the obedience of the Gentiles,
CSB17.19: by the power of miraculous signs and wonders, <sup>ⓑ</sup> and by the power of God’s Spirit. As a result, I have fully proclaimed the gospel of Christ from Jerusalem <sup>ⓒ</sup> all the way around to Illyricum. <sup>[9]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.20: My aim is to preach the gospel where Christ has not been named, <sup>ⓔ</sup> so that I will not build on someone else’s foundation, <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.21:
but, as it is written, Those who were not told about him will see,
and those who have not heard will understand. [10] ⓖ
CSB17.22: That is why I have been prevented many times from coming to you. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.23: But now I no longer have any work to do in these regions, <sup>[11]</sup> and I have strongly desired for many years to come to you <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.24: whenever I travel to Spain. <sup>[12]</sup> For I hope to see you when I pass through and to be assisted by you for my journey there, <sup>ⓙ</sup> once I have first enjoyed your company <sup>ⓚ</sup> for a while.
CSB17.25: Right now I am traveling to Jerusalem <sup>ⓛ</sup> to serve the saints, <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.26: because Macedonia <sup>ⓝ</sup> and Achaia <sup>ⓐ</sup> were pleased to make a contribution for the poor among the saints in Jerusalem.
CSB17.27: Yes, they were pleased, and indeed are indebted to them. For if the Gentiles have shared in their spiritual benefits, <sup>ⓑ</sup> then they are obligated to minister to them in material needs.
CSB17.28: So when I have finished this and safely delivered the funds <sup>[13]</sup> to them, <sup>[14]</sup> <sup>ⓒ</sup> I will visit you on the way to Spain.
CSB17.29: I know that when I come to you, I will come in the fullness of the blessing <sup>[15]</sup> of Christ.
CSB17.30: Now I appeal to you, brothers and sisters, through our Lord Jesus Christ and through the love of the Spirit, <sup>ⓓ</sup> to strive together with me in fervent prayers to God on my behalf. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.31: Pray that I may be rescued from the unbelievers in Judea, <sup>ⓕ</sup> that my ministry to <sup>[16]</sup> Jerusalem <sup>ⓖ</sup> may be acceptable to the saints, <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.32: and that, by God’s will, <sup>ⓘ</sup> I may come to you with joy <sup>ⓙ</sup> and be refreshed together with you.
CSB17.33: May the God of peace be with all of you. <sup>ⓚ</sup> Amen.

AMP.1: Now we who are strong [in our convictions and faith] ought to [patiently] put up with the weaknesses of those who are not strong, and not just please ourselves.
AMP.2: Let each one of us [make it a practice to] please his neighbor for his good, to build him up spiritually.
AMP.3: For even Christ did not please Himself; but as it is written [in Scripture], "The reproaches of those who reproached You (the Father) fell on Me (the Son)."
AMP.4: For whatever was written in earlier times was written for our instruction, so that through endurance and the encouragement of the Scriptures we might have hope and overflow with confidence in His promises.
AMP.5: Now may the God who gives endurance and who supplies encouragement grant that you be of the same mind with one another according to Christ Jesus,
AMP.6: so that with one accord you may with one voice glorify and praise and honor the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
AMP.7: Therefore, [continue to] accept and welcome one another, just as Christ has accepted and welcomed us to the glory of [our great] God.
AMP.8: For I tell you that Christ has become a servant and a minister to the circumcision (Jews) on behalf of God's truth, to confirm and verify the promises made to the fathers,
AMP.9:
and for the Gentiles to glorify God for His mercy [to them, since God had no covenant with them]. As it is written and forever remains written,
"Therefore I praise You among the Gentiles,
And sing praises to Your name."
AMP.10:
Again it says,
"Rejoice and celebrate, O Gentiles, along with His people."
AMP.11:
And again,
"Praise the Lord all you Gentiles,
And let all the peoples praise Him!"
AMP.12:
Again Isaiah says,
"There shall be a root ofJesse,
He who arises to rule [as King] over the Gentiles,
In Him shall the Gentiles hope."
AMP.13: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing [through the experience of your faith] that by the power of the Holy Spirit you will abound in hope and overflow with confidence in His promises.
AMP.14: Personally I am convinced about you, my brothers and sisters, that you yourselves are full of goodness, amply filled with all [spiritual] knowledge, and competent to admonish and counsel and instruct one another.
AMP.15: Still, on some points I have written to you very boldly and without reservation to remind you [about them] again, because of the grace that was given to me from God,
AMP.16: to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles. I minister as a priest the gospel of God, in order that my offering of the Gentiles may become acceptable [to Him], sanctified [made holy and set apart for His purpose] by the Holy Spirit.
AMP.17: In Christ Jesus, then, I have found [legitimate] reason for boasting in things related [to my service] to God.
AMP.18: For I will not [even] presume to speak of anything except what Christ has done through me [as an instrument in His hands], resulting in the obedience of the Gentiles [to the gospel], by word and deed,
AMP.19: with the power of signs and wonders, [and all of it] in the power of the Spirit. So [starting] from Jerusalem and as far away as Illyricum, I have fully preached the gospel [faithfully preaching the good news] of Christ [where it had not before been preached].
AMP.20: Accordingly I set a goal to preach the gospel, not where Christ's name was already known, so that I would not build on another man's foundation;
AMP.21:
but [instead I would act on this goal] as it is written [in Scripture],
"They who had no news of Him shall see,
And they who have not heard [of Him] shall understand."
AMP.22: This [goal—my commitment to this principle] is the reason why I have often been prevented from coming to you [in Rome].
AMP.23: But now, with no further place for work in these regions, and since I have longed for many years to come to you—
AMP.24: whenever I go [on my trip] to Spain—I hope to see you as I pass through [Rome], and to be helped on my journey there by you, after I have first enjoyed your company for a little while.
AMP.25: But for now, I am going to Jerusalem to serve the saints (Jewish believers).
AMP.26: For [Gentile believers in] Macedonia and Achaia have been pleased to make a contribution for the poor among the saints (Jewish believers) in Jerusalem.
AMP.27: They were pleased to do it, and they are indebted to them. For if the Gentiles have come to share in their spiritual things, then they are indebted to serve them also in [tangible] material things.
AMP.28: Therefore, when I have finished this [mission] and have safely given to them what has been raised, I will go on by way of you to Spain.
AMP.29: I know that when I do come to you, I will come in the abundant blessing of Christ.
AMP.30: I urge you, believers, by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the Spirit, to join together with me in your prayers to God in my behalf,
AMP.31: [and pray] that I may be rescued from the unbelievers in Judea, and that my service for Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints (Jewish believers) there;
AMP.32: so that by God's will I may come to you with joy and find rest in your company.
AMP.33: May the God of peace be with you all! Amen.

MSG.1: Those of us who are strong and able in the faith need to step in and lend a hand to those who falter, and not just do what is most convenient for us. Strength is for service, not status.
MSG.2: Each one of us needs to look after the good of the people around us, asking ourselves, "How can I help?"
MSG.3: That's exactly what Jesus did. He didn't make it easy for himself by avoiding people's troubles, but waded right in and helped out. "I took on the troubles of the troubled," is the way Scripture puts it.
MSG.4: Even if it was written in Scripture long ago, you can be sure it's written for us. God wants the combination of his steady, constant calling and warm, personal counsel in Scripture to come to characterize us, keeping us alert for whatever he will do next.
MSG.5: May our dependably steady and warmly personal God develop maturity in you so that you get along with each other as well as Jesus gets along with us all.
MSG.6: Then we'll be a choir—not our voices only, but our very lives singing in harmony in a stunning anthem to the God and Father of our Master Jesus!
MSG.7: So reach out and welcome one another to God's glory. Jesus did it; now you do it!
MSG.8: Jesus, staying true to God's purposes, reached out in a special way to the Jewish insiders so that the old ancestral promises would come true for them.
MSG.9: As a result, the non-Jewish outsiders have been able to experience mercy and to show appreciation to God. Just think of all the Scriptures that will come true in what we do! For instance: Then I'll join outsiders in a hymn-sing; I'll sing to your name!
MSG.10: And this one: Outsiders and insiders, rejoice together!
MSG.11: And again: People of all nations, celebrate God! All colors and races, give hearty praise!
MSG.12: And Isaiah's word: There's the root of our ancestor Jesse, breaking through the earth and growing tree tall, Tall enough for everyone everywhere to see and take hope!
MSG.13: Oh! May the God of green hope fill you up with joy, fill you up with peace, so that your believing lives, filled with the life-giving energy of the Holy Spirit, will brim over with hope!
MSG.14: Personally, I've been completely satisfied with who you are and what you are doing. You seem to me to be well-motivated and well-instructed, quite capable of guiding and advising one another.
MSG.15: So, my dear friends, don't take my rather bold and blunt language as criticism. It's not criticism. I'm simply underlining how very much I need your help in carrying out this highly focused assignment God gave me,
MSG.16: this priestly and gospel work of serving the spiritual needs of the non-Jewish outsiders so they can be presented as an acceptable offering to God, made whole and holy by God's Holy Spirit.
MSG.17: Looking back over what has been accomplished and what I have observed, I must say I am most pleased—in the context of Jesus, I'd even say proud, but only in that context.
MSG.18: I have no interest in giving you a chatty account of my adventures, only the wondrously powerful and transformingly present words and deeds of Christ in me that triggered a believing response among the outsiders.
MSG.19: In such ways I have trailblazed a preaching of the Message of Jesus all the way from Jerusalem far into northwestern Greece.
MSG.20: This has all been pioneer work, bringing the Message only into those places where Jesus was not yet known and worshiped.
MSG.21: My text has been, Those who were never told of him— they'll see him! Those who've never heard of him— they'll get the message!
MSG.22: And that's why it has taken me so long to finally get around to coming to you.
MSG.23: But now that there is no more pioneering work to be done in these parts, and since I have looked forward to seeing you for many years,
MSG.24: I'm planning my visit. I'm headed for Spain, and expect to stop off on the way to enjoy a good visit with you, and eventually have you send me off with God's blessing.
MSG.25: First, though, I'm going to Jerusalem to deliver a relief offering to the Christians there.
MSG.26: The Greeks—all the way from the Macedonians in the north to the Achaians in the south—decided they wanted to take up a collection for the poor among the believers in Jerusalem.
MSG.27: They were happy to do this, but it was also their duty. Seeing that they got in on all the spiritual gifts that flowed out of the Jerusalem community so generously, it is only right that they do what they can to relieve their poverty.
MSG.28: As soon as I have done this—personally handed over this "fruit basket"—I'm off to Spain, with a stopover with you in Rome.
MSG.29: My hope is that my visit with you is going to be one of Christ's more extravagant blessings.
MSG.30: I have one request, dear friends: Pray for me. Pray strenuously with and for me—to God the Father, through the power of our Master Jesus, through the love of the Spirit—
MSG.31: that I will be delivered from the lions' den of unbelievers in Judea. Pray also that my relief offering to the Jerusalem Christians will be accepted in the spirit in which it is given.
MSG.32: Then, God willing, I'll be on my way to you with a light and eager heart, looking forward to being refreshed by your company.
MSG.33: God's peace be with all of you. Oh, yes!

NIV.1: We who are strong ought to bear with the failings of the weak and not to please ourselves.
NIV.2: Each of us should please his neighbor for his good, to build him up.
NIV.3: For even Christ did not please himself but, as it is written: “The insults of those who insult you have fallen on me.”
NIV.4: For everything that was written in the past was written to teach us, so that through endurance and the encouragement of the Scriptures we might have hope.
NIV.5: May the God who gives endurance and encouragement give you a spirit of unity among yourselves as you follow Christ Jesus,
NIV.6: so that with one heart and mouth you may glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NIV.7: Accept one another, then, just as Christ accepted you, in order to bring praise to God.
NIV.8: For I tell you that Christ has become a servant of the Jews on behalf of God’s truth, to confirm the promises made to the patriarchs
NIV.9:
so that the Gentiles may glorify God for his mercy, as it is written:
“Therefore I will praise you among the Gentiles;
I will sing hymns to your name.”
NIV.10:
Again, it says,
“Rejoice, O Gentiles, with his people.”
NIV.11:
And again,
“Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles,
and sing praises to him, all you peoples.”
NIV.12:
And again, Isaiah says,
“The Root of Jesse will spring up,
one who will arise to rule over the nations;
the Gentiles will hope in him.”
NIV.13: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace as you trust in him, so that you may overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
NIV.14:
Paul the Minister to the Gentiles
I myself am convinced, my brothers, that you yourselves are full of goodness, complete in knowledge and competent to instruct one another.
NIV.15: I have written you quite boldly on some points, as if to remind you of them again, because of the grace God gave me
NIV.16: to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles with the priestly duty of proclaiming the gospel of God, so that the Gentiles might become an offering acceptable to God, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.
NIV.17: Therefore I glory in Christ Jesus in my service to God.
NIV.18: I will not venture to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me in leading the Gentiles to obey God by what I have said and done—
NIV.19: by the power of signs and miracles, through the power of the Spirit. So from Jerusalem all the way around to Illyricum, I have fully proclaimed the gospel of Christ.
NIV.20: It has always been my ambition to preach the gospel where Christ was not known, so that I would not be building on someone else’s foundation.
NIV.21:
Rather, as it is written:
“Those who were not told about him will see,
and those who have not heard will understand.”
NIV.22: This is why I have often been hindered from coming to you.
NIV.23:
Paul’s Plan to Visit Rome
But now that there is no more place for me to work in these regions, and since I have been longing for many years to see you,
NIV.24: I plan to do so when I go to Spain. I hope to visit you while passing through and to have you assist me on my journey there, after I have enjoyed your company for a while.
NIV.25: Now, however, I am on my way to Jerusalem in the service of the saints there.
NIV.26: For Macedonia and Achaia were pleased to make a contribution for the poor among the saints in Jerusalem.
NIV.27: They were pleased to do it, and indeed they owe it to them. For if the Gentiles have shared in the Jews’ spiritual blessings, they owe it to the Jews to share with them their material blessings.
NIV.28: So after I have completed this task and have made sure that they have received this fruit, I will go to Spain and visit you on the way.
NIV.29: I know that when I come to you, I will come in the full measure of the blessing of Christ.
NIV.30: I urge you, brothers, by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the Spirit, to join me in my struggle by praying to God for me.
NIV.31: Pray that I may be rescued from the unbelievers in Judea and that my service in Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints there,
NIV.32: so that by God’s will I may come to you with joy and together with you be refreshed.
NIV.33: The God of peace be with you all. Amen.

NRSVCE.1: We who are strong ought to put up with the failings of the weak, and not to please ourselves.
NRSVCE.2: Each of us must please our neighbor for the good purpose of building up the neighbor.
NRSVCE.3: For Christ did not please himself; but, as it is written, “The insults of those who insult you have fallen on me.”
NRSVCE.4: For whatever was written in former days was written for our instruction, so that by steadfastness and by the encouragement of the scriptures we might have hope.
NRSVCE.5: May the God of steadfastness and encouragement grant you to live in harmony with one another, in accordance with Christ Jesus,
NRSVCE.6: so that together you may with one voice glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.7: Welcome one another, therefore, just as Christ has welcomed you, for the glory of God.
NRSVCE.8: For I tell you that Christ has become a servant of the circumcised on behalf of the truth of God in order that he might confirm the promises given to the patriarchs,
NRSVCE.9:
and in order that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, “Therefore I will confess you among the Gentiles,
and sing praises to your name”;
NRSVCE.10: and again he says, “Rejoice, O Gentiles, with his people”;
NRSVCE.11:
and again, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles,
and let all the peoples praise him”;
NRSVCE.12:
and again Isaiah says, “The root of Jesse shall come,
the one who rises to rule the Gentiles;
in him the Gentiles shall hope.”
NRSVCE.13: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that you may abound in hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
NRSVCE.14: I myself feel confident about you, my brothers and sisters, that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and able to instruct one another.
NRSVCE.15: Nevertheless on some points I have written to you rather boldly by way of reminder, because of the grace given me by God
NRSVCE.16: to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles in the priestly service of the gospel of God, so that the offering of the Gentiles may be acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.
NRSVCE.17: In Christ Jesus, then, I have reason to boast of my work for God.
NRSVCE.18: For I will not venture to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me to win obedience from the Gentiles, by word and deed,
NRSVCE.19: by the power of signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit of God, so that from Jerusalem and as far around as Illyricum I have fully proclaimed the good news of Christ.
NRSVCE.20: Thus I make it my ambition to proclaim the good news, not where Christ has already been named, so that I do not build on someone else’s foundation,
NRSVCE.21:
but as it is written, “Those who have never been told of him shall see,
and those who have never heard of him shall understand.”
NRSVCE.22: This is the reason that I have so often been hindered from coming to you.
NRSVCE.23: But now, with no further place for me in these regions, I desire, as I have for many years, to come to you
NRSVCE.24: when I go to Spain. For I do hope to see you on my journey and to be sent on by you, once I have enjoyed your company for a little while.
NRSVCE.25: At present, however, I am going to Jerusalem in a ministry to the saints;
NRSVCE.26: for Macedonia and Achaia have been pleased to share their resources with the poor among the saints at Jerusalem.
NRSVCE.27: They were pleased to do this, and indeed they owe it to them; for if the Gentiles have come to share in their spiritual blessings, they ought also to be of service to them in material things.
NRSVCE.28: So, when I have completed this, and have delivered to them what has been collected, I will set out by way of you to Spain;
NRSVCE.29: and I know that when I come to you, I will come in the fullness of the blessing of Christ.
NRSVCE.30: I appeal to you, brothers and sisters, by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the Spirit, to join me in earnest prayer to God on my behalf,
NRSVCE.31: that I may be rescued from the unbelievers in Judea, and that my ministry to Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints,
NRSVCE.32: so that by God’s will I may come to you with joy and be refreshed in your company.
NRSVCE.33: The God of peace be with all of you. Amen.

NET.1: But we who are strong ought to bear with the failings of the weak, and not just please ourselves.
NET.2: Let each of us please his neighbor for his good to build him up.
NET.3: For even Christ did not please himself, but just as it is written, “The insults of those who insult you have fallen on me.”
NET.4: For everything that was written in former times was written for our instruction, so that through endurance and through encouragement of the scriptures we may have hope.
NET.5: Now may the God of endurance and comfort give you unity with one another in accordance with Christ Jesus,
NET.6: so that together you may with one voice glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NET.7: Receive one another, then, just as Christ also received you, to God’s glory.
NET.8: For I tell you that Christ has become a servant of the circumcised on behalf of God’s truth to confirm the promises made to the fathers,
NET.9: and thus the Gentiles glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, “Because of this I will confess you among the Gentiles, and I will sing praises to your name.”
NET.10: And again it says: “Rejoice, O Gentiles, with his people.”
NET.11: And again, “Praise the Lord all you Gentiles, and let all the peoples praise him.”
NET.12: And again Isaiah says, “The root of Jesse will come, and the one who rises to rule over the Gentiles, in him will the Gentiles hope.”
NET.13: Now may the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace as you believe in him, so that you may abound in hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
NET.14: But I myself am fully convinced about you, my brothers and sisters, that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and able to instruct one another.
NET.15: But I have written more boldly to you on some points so as to remind you, because of the grace given to me by God
NET.16: to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles. I serve the gospel of God like a priest, so that the Gentiles may become an acceptable offering, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.
NET.17: So I boast in Christ Jesus about the things that pertain to God.
NET.18: For I will not dare to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me in order to bring about the obedience of the Gentiles, by word and deed,
NET.19: in the power of signs and wonders, in the power of the Spirit of God. So from Jerusalem even as far as Illyricum I have fully preached the gospel of Christ.
NET.20: And in this way I desire to preach where Christ has not been named, so as not to build on another person’s foundation,
NET.21: but as it is written: “Those who were not told about him will see, and those who have not heard will understand.”
NET.22: This is the reason I was often hindered from coming to you.
NET.23: But now there is nothing more to keep me in these regions, and I have for many years desired to come to you
NET.24: when I go to Spain. For I hope to visit you when I pass through and that you will help me on my journey there, after I have enjoyed your company for a while.
NET.25: But now I go to Jerusalem to minister to the saints.
NET.26: For Macedonia and Achaia are pleased to make some contribution for the poor among the saints in Jerusalem.
NET.27: For they were pleased to do this, and indeed they are indebted to the Jerusalem saints. For if the Gentiles have shared in their spiritual things, they are obligated also to minister to them in material things.
NET.28: Therefore after I have completed this and have safely delivered this bounty to them, I will set out for Spain by way of you,
NET.29: and I know that when I come to you I will come in the fullness of Christ’s blessing.
NET.30: Now I urge you, brothers and sisters, through our Lord Jesus Christ and through the love of the Spirit, to join fervently with me in prayer to God on my behalf.
NET.31: Pray that I may be rescued from those who are disobedient in Judea and that my ministry in Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints,
NET.32: so that by God’s will I may come to you with joy and be refreshed in your company.
NET.33: Now may the God of peace be with all of you. Amen.
